entries.d: a bird that flies and hunts at night
entries.s: At night, you can hear the _____ with big round eyes hooting from the tall tree.

entries.d: a small, colourful bird people keep at home
entries.s: My small blue pet _____ in its cage can say 'hello' and 'goodbye'.

entries.d: a very big grey animal with a long nose
entries.s: The huge grey _____ at the zoo has very big flappy ears and a long trunk.

entries.d: a small animal with eight legs
entries.s: There is a black _____ with eight legs and its sticky web in the corner of the ceiling.

entries.d: an animal that flies at night
entries.s: A _____ with leathery wings sleeps hanging upside-down during the day and flies at night.

entries.d: a big, dangerous fish in the sea
entries.s: The dangerous _____ with sharp teeth swims fast through the ocean looking for fish.

entries.d: an animal with black and white stripes
entries.s: A _____ looks like a horse but has black and white stripes all over its body.

entries.d: a big animal with a hump on its back
entries.s: In the hot desert, people ride a _____ with one or two humps on its back and long eyelashes.

entries.d: a small horse
entries.s: The small children ride a little _____ — a small horse — at the farm on Sundays.

entries.d: a small, soft pet with no tail
entries.s: My small furry pet _____ loves to eat carrots and lettuce in its cage and squeaks for food.

entries.d: a pink farm animal with a curly tail
entries.s: The pink _____ on the muddy farm likes to roll in the wet mud and has a curly tail.

entries.d: a small animal with long ears
entries.s: Our pet _____ has soft white fur, long floppy ears, and hops around the garden.

entries.d: a small animal with four legs and a long tail
entries.s: A small green _____ with scales sits on the warm rock in the sun and flicks its tongue.

entries.d: an animal like a big mouse
entries.s: The big _____ has a long bald pink tail and sharp front teeth. It eats food from the bin.

entries.d: a very small animal with a long tail
entries.s: A little grey _____ with a thin tail ran quickly across the kitchen floor and hid behind the fridge.

entries.d: a slow animal with a hard shell
entries.s: My pet _____ walks very very slowly through the garden with its shell on its back.

entries.d: a container with four sides and a top
entries.s: Put the heavy books in that big cardboard _____ with a lid.

entries.d: a glass container for fish or reptiles
entries.s: My pet fish swim around in a big glass _____ filled with water and green plants.

entries.d: a space with bars to keep animals in
entries.s: The yellow canary bird sings in its metal _____ with thin bars all morning.

entries.d: a glass box for lizards or snakes
entries.s: The pet lizard lives in a warm glass _____ with sand, rocks, and a heat lamp.

entries.d: not normal, different from most things
entries.s: A pet snake is a very _____ animal to have at home. Most families have cats or dogs instead.

entries.d: once every twenty-four hours
entries.s: I brush my teeth two times _____ — once in the morning and once before bed.

entries.d: one time
entries.s: He feeds his cat _____ a day in the morning at 7am with dry food.

entries.d: two times
entries.s: She walks her pet dog _____ every day — one time before school and one time after school.

entries.d: from one side to the other, everywhere in
entries.s: There are many pets in family homes _____ Britain, from Scotland in the north to England in the south.

entries.d: not safe, can hurt you
entries.s: Wild lions are _____ animals with big teeth and claws; don't go near them at the zoo!

entries.d: a place where people grow food and keep animals
entries.s: The cows, chickens, pigs and sheep all live together on the big green countryside _____.

entries.d: not far from, close to
entries.s: Our house is _____ the school. I can walk there in just five minutes.

entries.d: paper with news you read every day
entries.s: Dad reads the daily _____ with news stories and photos at breakfast every morning.

entries.d: once every seven days
entries.s: We have swimming lessons three times _____ — every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

entries.d: a container to carry things in
entries.s: She puts the picked apples in a big woven _____ with a handle on top.

entries.d: a girl child of a parent
entries.s: Mr and Mrs Smith have one son called Tom and one _____ called Anna.

entries.d: to move a car from one place to another
entries.s: My dad can _____ us in his car to the zoo on Sunday. He has a driving licence.

entries.d: all people, every person
entries.s: _____ — all 25 pupils — in our class has a pet at home. Not a single child is without one.

entries.d: not near, a long distance from here
entries.s: Grandpa lives _____ in another city that is 300 km away, so we always go by car.

entries.d: your father's or mother's father
entries.s: My _____ — my dad's father — tells us funny stories from his childhood every Sunday.

entries.d: the woman who is your parent
entries.s: Her _____ takes her to school every morning in the car. She is a woman and a parent.

entries.d: a sound, often loud or strange
entries.s: What is that loud banging _____? I can hear something strange coming from the cellar.

entries.d: to not leave, to be in one place
entries.s: Can we _____ here a little longer at the playground, please? I don't want to go home yet.

entries.d: a soft toy animal for children
entries.s: She sleeps with her favourite soft _____ bear every night. She has had it since she was a baby.

entries.d: to go and see a person or place
entries.s: We _____ our elderly grandma at her house every weekend and have tea with her.

entries.d: to want to know more about something
entries.s: He is _____ wild animals — he reads books about them and watches nature shows on TV.

entries.d: the soft hair on an animal's body
entries.s: The cat has soft, fluffy white _____ all over its body. I like to stroke it.

entries.d: about one person, private
entries.s: Don't put your _____ information like your address or phone number on the internet.

entries.d: the person an animal or thing belongs to
entries.s: The lost dog runs back to its _____ — the person who bought it and feeds it — in the park.

entries.d: your parent's sister
entries.s: My _____ Jane — my mother's sister — always brings presents for me when she visits our house.

entries.d: a word to start a letter
entries.s: _____ Grandma, thank you for the lovely birthday card you sent me. Love, Anna.

entries.d: a message you write on paper and send
entries.s: I write a long _____ on paper with a pen to my friend in London and put it in an envelope.

entries.d: to use your teeth on something
entries.s: Be careful around that angry dog! It has sharp teeth and can _____ you!

entries.d: the start, the first part
entries.s: The _____ of the book is very funny — the first chapter makes me laugh out loud.

entries.d: to start doing something
entries.s: The English lessons _____ at eight o'clock in the morning. We start at that time.

entries.d: a friendly way to end a letter
entries.s: _____, Tom. [at the end of a letter]

entries.d: the last part of something
entries.s: The _____ of the film is very sad. In the last scene the hero dies.

entries.d: to must have something
entries.s: My pet rabbit is hungry; I _____ to buy more food because we have run out.
